title: SLA Tracking
cert_objective: CySA+ CS0-003 Domain 4 — Reporting and Communication
scenario:
AEGIS TERMINAL v1.0 -- VERIDIAN SYSTEMS SOC

INCOMING TICKET -- OPERATIONS TEAM
PRIORITY: MEDIUM
ANALYST: YOU

OPERATION IRONCLAD -- SLA PERFORMANCE REVIEW

The operations manager needs a SLA adherence report for the past 3 weeks.
Six incident tickets have been reviewed. Each ticket has a priority-based
SLA target (time from opening to resolution).

SLA TARGETS BY PRIORITY:
  Critical:  4 hours
  High:      24 hours
  Medium:    72 hours
  Low:       120 hours

TICKET HISTORY:

  TKT-001  CRITICAL  opened:2026-04-01 08:00  resolved:2026-04-01 10:00
  TKT-002  HIGH      opened:2026-04-02 09:00  resolved:2026-04-03 11:00
  TKT-003  MEDIUM    opened:2026-04-03 14:00  resolved:2026-04-07 14:00
  TKT-004  CRITICAL  opened:2026-04-05 22:00  resolved:2026-04-06 06:00
  TKT-005  HIGH      opened:2026-04-08 10:00  resolved:2026-04-09 06:00
  TKT-006  LOW       opened:2026-04-10 11:00  resolved:2026-04-20 11:00

Type 'tools' to compute elapsed time per ticket and flag SLA breaches.

What is the SLA adherence rate as a percentage?
challenge: What is the SLA adherence rate (percentage of tickets resolved within SLA)?
hints:
Go to https://www.atlassian.com/incident-management/incident-response/sla
        Read about SLA (Service Level Agreement) in incident response.
        SLA adherence rate is a key metric reported to management -- it
        measures whether the team is meeting its response commitments.

For each ticket: calculate elapsed hours from 'opened' to 'resolved'.
        Compare elapsed to the SLA target for that priority.
        If elapsed <= SLA hours: ticket MET its SLA.
        If elapsed > SLA hours: ticket BREACHED its SLA.
        Adherence rate = MET count / total count x 100.
        Type 'tools' to compute elapsed times for all 6 tickets.

Type 'tools' in the game. The SLA tracker computes elapsed time for
        each ticket, flags [MET] or [BREACHED], and calculates adherence rate.
        Count the [MET] tickets and divide by 6.

SPOILER -- Only 2 tickets met their SLA:
        TKT-001: 2h elapsed, 4h SLA -> MET
        TKT-005: 20h elapsed, 24h SLA -> MET
        The other 4 are BREACHED. 2/6 = 33%.
        TKT-004 (critical) took 8h but had a 4h SLA -- a midnight incident
        that missed the critical response window.
        Type: 33%
tools: Computes elapsed resolution time per ticket, compares to priority-based SLA targets, flags each ticket as [MET] or [BREACHED], and calculates overall adherence rate.
learn:
SLA (Service Level Agreement) tracking measures whether the security team is
meeting its response commitments. SLA adherence is reported to management,
auditors, and sometimes clients.

SLA in incident response:

Priority tiers and typical SLA targets:
  Critical (P1):  4 hours -- system down, active breach, immediate business impact
  High (P2):      24 hours -- significant impact, no workaround available
  Medium (P3):    72 hours (3 days) -- moderate impact, workaround available
  Low (P4):       120 hours (5 days) -- minimal impact, informational

Why SLAs matter:
  Legal/contractual: MSP/MSSP contracts may include financial penalties for SLA breach
  Operational: SLA adherence measures team capacity and process efficiency
  Regulatory: some frameworks (SOC 2, ISO 27001) require defined response times

SLA breach root causes:
  Staffing gaps: insufficient analysts during off-hours (TKT-004: midnight critical)
  Alert fatigue: too many tickets for the team size
  Escalation failures: ticket not routed to correct team

Improving SLA adherence:
  On-call rotations for critical incidents (24/7 coverage)
  SOAR automation for common incident types
  SLA monitoring dashboards with proactive alerts before breach

steps.question: How many hours did TKT-004 (CRITICAL) take to resolve, and did it meet or breach its 4-hour SLA?
hints: Count the hours from 22:00 to 06:00: that crosses midnight. 22:00 to 00:00 = 2h, plus 00:00 to 06:00 = 6h. Total: 8 hours.
The CRITICAL SLA target is 4 hours. TKT-004 took 8 hours — double the SLA window.
TKT-004 BREACHED its SLA (8h elapsed vs. 4h target). A midnight critical incident with insufficient overnight coverage.

wrong_answer_hints.response: TKT-004 took 8 hours but the critical SLA is only 4 hours — it missed the target by 4 hours.
8 hours is the elapsed time, but the question asks whether the ticket met or breached its SLA. 8h > 4h SLA = BREACHED.

steps.question: How many of the 6 tickets met their SLA targets?
hints: Check each ticket: elapsed time must be less than or equal to the SLA target for that priority to count as MET.
TKT-001: 2h vs 4h SLA = MET. TKT-002: 26h vs 24h SLA = BREACHED. TKT-003: 96h vs 72h SLA = BREACHED.
TKT-004: 8h vs 4h SLA = BREACHED. TKT-005: 20h vs 24h SLA = MET. TKT-006: 240h vs 120h SLA = BREACHED.
Only TKT-001 (2h, critical) and TKT-005 (20h, high) met their SLA targets. That is 2 out of 6.

wrong_answer_hints.response: Check TKT-002 again: it opened 2026-04-02 09:00 and resolved 2026-04-03 11:00 — that is 26 hours, breaching the 24h HIGH SLA.
Only 2 tickets met SLA. TKT-002 took 26h (24h SLA = BREACH), TKT-003 took 96h (72h SLA = BREACH), TKT-004 took 8h (4h SLA = BREACH), TKT-006 took 240h (120h SLA = BREACH).

steps.question: What is the SLA adherence rate (percentage of tickets resolved within SLA)?
hints: SLA adherence rate = (number of tickets that MET SLA / total tickets) × 100.
2 tickets met their SLA out of 6 total tickets.
SLA adherence rate is 33%. Only TKT-001 and TKT-005 resolved within their SLA windows.

wrong_answer_hints.response: 67% would mean 4 tickets met SLA — but only 2 did. Adherence = MET / total = 2/6 = 33%.
50% would mean 3 tickets met SLA. Only 2 did (TKT-001 and TKT-005). The rate is 33%.

debrief.summary: SLA adherence rate is 33% -- only 2 of 6 tickets resolved within SLA. TKT-004 is the most notable breach: a critical incident opened at 22:00 took 8 hours but had a 4-hour SLA. This indicates insufficient overnight coverage for critical incidents. TKT-006 (low priority) ran 240 hours against a 120-hour SLA -- low-priority tickets are being deprioritized excessively.
debrief.real_world: SLA adherence below 50% is a significant operational concern. MSSP contracts often include financial SLA penalties -- a 33% adherence rate would result in contractual penalties in a managed service environment. The TKT-004 pattern (midnight critical with no coverage) is a common finding in organizations without formal on-call programs.
debrief.next_step:
PagerDuty on on-call management for security teams:
https://www.pagerduty.com/resources/learn/incident-response-on-call/
debrief.cert_link:
CySA+ CS0-003 Domain 4 -- Reporting and Communication:
"Given a scenario, track SLA adherence and generate reports demonstrating security team performance against defined response time targets."
debrief.exam_tip: On the exam, SLA adherence questions test: (1) SLA calculation (elapsed time vs. target), (2) MET vs. BREACHED classification (elapsed <= SLA = MET), and (3) adherence rate (MET / total x 100). Know that SLA targets are priority-based (critical = hours, low = days). Know that SLA adherence is an operational metric reported to management.
